observations.recommendation: 6. We recommended that the management mark all motor vehicles of the Municipality with “For Official Use Only” and to exert an effort that existing motor vehicles which are not yet government plated be registered accordingly under which place the name of the Municipality.

We reiterate this recommendation that the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish at the year a certified list
of taxpayers with amount due and collectible for the year to the
Municipal Accountant based on the Real Property Tax Account
Register/ Taxpayer’s Index Card and make an effort to collect all
taxes due to the LGU.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use this as a basis in recording
Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax
Receivable; adjust the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax
to present a more reliable and accurate balance.

We recommended that the agency should create an Inventory Committee
to conduct an actual physical count of all its PPE by listing all the PPE
items whether included in the latest Report on Physical Count of PPE/ Physical
Inventory Report or not and compare it to all PPE Ledger Cards. This will be the
basis in preparing the Physical Inventory Plan. Further, reconcile the balances
derived by the Inventory Committee Report with the Accounting Records and
take appropriate actions depending on the status of the PPE. Further, apply the
procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs pursuant to COA
Circular 2020-006 or the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of
Government Agencies.

We recommended that the Property Custodian regularly renew/update
the PARs of the PPEs every three years or as soon as there is a change in
custody of accountability to strengthen control and accountability over the
government assets.

We therefore recommended that the Management conduct an extensive
review and adjustment of the PPE and Inventory Accounts of the Agency
and adopt the provisions of the COA Circular 2022-004. Future
procurement must also be in line with the said Circular.

We recommended the Management to instruct the Accountable Officer to
(a) maintain the records and registers as cited under the Revised Cash
Examination Manual and as presented in the Volume II of the GAM for
more effective and efficient monitoring and control of funds and for
proper accounting and auditing of related accounts thereafter in lieu of
the cashbooks being maintained; and (b) to record all transactions of cash
in bank including receipts and issuances of checks, record cancelled
checks and debit and credit memos in the checkbooks.

We therefore recommended that the Accountable Officer should prepare
and regularly submit the Monthly Report of Accountability for
Accountable Forms with and without money value to the Audit Team as
prescribed under Section 17, Chapter 7, Volume I of the GAM for LGUs
to ensure compliance with the afore-cited regulation and to facilitate
inventory of accountable forms.

We therefore recommended that the management to include the Audit
Team in the acquisition, delivery, inspection and acceptance activities of
the agency and to submit the necessary inventory reports to the Office of
the Auditor within the reglementary period.

We recommended that the management instruct the BAC and
Procurement Support Unit to stop the practice of submitting the contract
and supporting documents together with the related disbursement
vouchers, instead furnished the Auditor copy of the contract and other
supporting documents not later than five (5) working days from issuance
of purchase orders and perfection of contracts as required under Section
3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the
copies of MOA/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the NGAs to
evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose and
as required under Item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June
14, 2012.

We recommended that management should:
a. At the start of the year, submit to the Auditor’s Office a complete list of
all on-going projects, programs and activities and those that are to be
implemented during the year and should be updated every quarter
following the format prescribed by COA Circular 2013-004, dated
January 13, 2014; and
b. Based on the data sourced from monthly monitoring report prepared by
the agency and verified by the Technical Audit Specialist of the
Commission, the project status in Annex A of COA Cirular 2013-004
should be maintain as current as possible and updated using the schedule
stated above.

We reiterate our recommendations that the management mark all motor
vehicles of the Municipality with “For Official Use Only” and to exert an
effort that existing motor vehicles which are not yet government plated be
registered accordingly under which place the name of the Municipality.

We therefore recommended that the management conduct planning
conferences to ensure that all the buildings and other structures of the
Agency are friendly to differently-abled persons/PWDs, and include the
same in its budget pursuant to BP Blg 344 in order to address their rights
to participate fully in the social life and the development of the societies
within the Agency.

We recommended that Management make representation with the
Sangguniang Bayan for the creation and installation of the Internal Audit
Service (IAS) directly under the Office of the Municipal Mayor to comply
with the requirement of AO Nos. 278 and 70 and ensure the quality of
performance and compliance with laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management should require the Municipal
Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that
require insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS and
furnished a copy to the Audit Team for review and verification.
Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of
loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic
Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by
PD No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

We recommended that the Management directs the completion of the
conduct of the annual physical count of PPEs and Inventories and come
up with the RPCPPE and RPCI and accordingly prepare the PIF to be
submitted to the Audit Team and GSIS to secure the inclusion in the GIF.

We recommended that the LGU establish GAD database to contain sex
disaggregated data to serve as source of information for the conduct of
gender analysis, identify gender issues and determine gender responsive
PPAs and incorporate them in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.

1. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require all the depository banks to strictly comply with the submission of the mandated bank statements (BS) and supporting documents for all depository accounts maintained by the Municipality within five days after the end of each month. In turn, within ten days from the receipts of such BS, the same should be reconciled with the General Ledgers which shall be the basis for preparation of BRS; and henceforth, the BRS must be regularly submitted to the Auditor with Bank Statements and/or Snapshots within ten days after the end of each month together with the paid checks and debit/credit memos. Likewise, prepare the necessary journal entry voucher (JEVs) to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.

7. We reiterate this recommendation that the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish at the year a certified list of taxpayers with amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/ Taxpayer’s Index Card and make an effort to collect all taxes due to the LGU.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use this as a basis in recording Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable; adjust the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax to present a more reliable and accurate balance.

9. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, direct the Municipal Accountant to timely submit the Statement of Receipts and Disbursements and related supporting documents to the source agencies to avoid non-reconciliation of balances as well as to avoid the risk that they may not be able to be given additional funding from the National Government.

10. We therefore recommended that the management to include the Audit Team in the acquisition, delivery, inspection and acceptance activities of the agency and to submit the necessary inventory reports to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.

11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the personnel concerned to submit the list of PPAs of the Municipality, annually at the beginning of the year; and prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure projects in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

12. Formulate a policy on the granting of FA to systematically determine the extent of aid to be given to individuals in need. Beneficiaries should be properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents.
13. Coordinate with the MSWO to assess request for medical and hospitalization assistance by way of Social Case Study Reports so that only the deserving indigents can be assisted and served.
14. Instruct the MSWO to require the applicants for AICS a complete submission of supporting documents for the assistance being requested as a basis of assessment and recommendation to make sure that only the deserving ones were provided with the necessary assistance to help them cope during crisis situations.

15. We recommended that the Management should instruct the HRMO Department to submit current plantilla of personnel every year and furnish the Audit Team copies of agency personnel movements (i.e. approved appointment/contract of service of every newly hired, promoted as well as the newly renewed contract) beforehand.

16. We recommended that Management should require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS and furnished a copy to the Audit Team for review and verification. Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

17. Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare on time the annual GPB for review and approval by DILG and submit the approved copy and the corresponding AR to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and
18. Approve charges and disbursements on GAD Fund only if supported with DILG reviewed and approved GPB to ascertain validity and propriety of disbursements.

19. Furnish a copy of the 20% DF report to the Office of the Auditor and ensure that the utilization of the 20% DF be limited to PPAs that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in accordance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1 and refrain from appropriating or including in the appropriation of development projects the personnel services, not allowable to be charged to the 20% DF.

24. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the designation and bonding of revenue collectors, as prescribed under Section 305 (f) of RA 7160 and Section 65, Volume I of the GAAM. In the meantime, those permanent and properly bonded employees should be directed to take over the issuance of CTCs and Official Receipts and collection of fees and charges.

25. The Budget Officer should maintain the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation for each class of expenditure as prescribed to ensure the preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and establish a strong internal control on obligation of expenses.

5. the Municipal Accountant obtain the necessary data from the Municipal Engineer, then prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger for Local Road Network, provide depreciation separately to each component of the road network, submit a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation, and disclose the road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2015-008;
17.We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require all the depository
banks to strictly comply with the submission of the mandated bank statements (BS)
and supporting documents for all depository accounts maintained by the
Municipality within five days after the end of each month. In turn, within ten days
from the receipts of such BS, the same should be reconciled with the General
Ledgers which shall be the basis for preparation of BRS; and henceforth, the BRS
must be regularly submitted to the Auditor with Bank Statements and/or Snapshots
within ten days after the end of each month together with the paid checks and
debit/credit memos. Likewise, prepare the necessary journal entry voucher (JEVs)
to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.

We recommended the Management to require:
a. The Inventory Committee to conduct thorough review and physical count of
the inventories and PPE by establishing its actual conditions and
whereabouts, and come up with the RPCI and RPCPPE;
b. The Inventory Committee to adhere with the submission of RPCI for the first
and second semesters not later than July 31 and January 31, respectively,
and the annual RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to the Audit
Team for subsequent review;
c. The Supply and Property Unit and Accounting Office to strengthen its
system of maintaining its accounting and property records such as SCs, PCs,
SLCs, PPELCs, RPCI and RPCPPE to facilitate periodic reconciliation of
these records to ensure reliability of the inventories and PPE account
balances as presented in the Financial Statements of the Municipality.
d. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for
the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer
should ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated
Property Card for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus
facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the
basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation
expenses pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its
appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to
present fairly the PPE accounts which its corresponding Accumulated
Depreciation Expense Accounts in FS.

We recommended that the management mark all motor vehicles of the
Municipality with “For Official Use Only” and to exert an effort that existing motor
vehicles which are not yet government plated be registered accordingly under which
place the name of the Municipality.

We reiterate this recommendation that the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish at the year a certified list of
taxpayers with amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal
Accountant based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/ Taxpayer’s
Index Card and make an effort to collect all taxes due to the LGU.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use this as a basis in recording Real
Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable; adjust the
Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax to present a more reliable and
accurate balance.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, direct the Municipal
Accountant to timely submit the Statement of Receipts and Disbursements and
related supporting documents to the source agencies to avoid non-reconciliation of
balances as well as to avoid the risk that they may not be able to be given additional
funding from the National Government.

We therefore recommended that the management to include the Audit Team
in the acquisition, delivery, inspection and acceptance activities of the agency and
the submit the necessary inventory reports to the Office of the Auditor within the
reglementary period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the personnel
concerned to submit the list of PPAs of the Municipality, annually at the beginning
of the year; and prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure
projects in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that Management should:
a. Formulate a policy on the granting of FA systematically determine the
extent of aid to be given to individuals in need. Beneficiaries should be
properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents.
b. Coordinate with the MSWO to assess request for medical and
hospitalization assistance by way of Social Case Study Reports so that
only the deserving indigents can be assisted and served.
c. Instruct the MSWO to require the applicants for AICS a complete
submission of supporting documents for the assistance being
requested as a basis of assessment and recommendation to make sure
that only the deserving ones were provided with the necessary
assistance to help them cope during crisis situations.

We recommended that the Management should instruct the HRMO
Department to submit current plantilla of personnel every year and furnish the
Audit Team copies of agency personnel movements (i.e. approved
appointment/contract of service of every newly hired, promoted as well as the newly
renewed contract) beforehand.

We recommended that Management should require the Municipal
Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require
insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS and furnished a copy to the
Audit Team for review and verification.
Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to
fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No. 656,
otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245 dated July
13, 1973.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare on time the annual GPB for
review and approval by DILG and submit the approved copy and the
corresponding AR to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period
provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and
b. Approve charges and disbursements on GAD Fund only if supported with
DILG reviewed and approved GPB to ascertain validity and propriety of
disbursements.

Furnish a copy of the 20% DF report to the Office of the Auditor and ensure
that the utilization of the 20% DF be limited to PPAs that partake the nature of
investment or capital expenditures in accordance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1
and refrain from appropriating or including in the appropriation of development
projects the personnel services, not allowable to be charged to the 20% DF.

1. We recommended that the required monthly fund utilization reports and status of implementation of PPAs be immediately posted in the LGU’s website and in at least least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality.
2. We recommended that Management submit the distribution lists for verification and validation purposes.

3. Constitute an Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.
4. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to ensure correctness.
6. the Municipal Mayor constitute a committee to conduct inventory of all road networks of the Municipality;
7. the Municipal Mayor fill-up the position of Supply Officer or create the position of GSO; and
8. the Municipal Engineer provide the local accountant and the Supply Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year as required under Section 19 (b) and 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.
10. Municipal Engineer and the MPDC install signboards at the project sites containing complete information;
11. the Municipal Engineer inform the Office of the Auditor when project signboard was already installed; and
12.the Municipal Accountant will require as supporting documents the picture of signboards being placed in the construction site for claims of mobilization expenses.

13.We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the designation and bonding of revenue collectors, as prescribed under Section 305 (f) of RA 7160 and Section 65, Volume I of the GAAM. In the meantime, those permanent and properly bonded employees should be directed to take over the issuance of CTCs and Official Receipts and collection of fees and charges.

14.We recommended that the LDRRMO submit to the NDRRMC through the OCD the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF on or before the 5th day following the end of each month and furnish copy to the audit team for audit/evaluation.

15.The Budget Officer should maintain the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation for each class of expenditure as prescribed to ensure the preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and establish a strong internal control on obligation of expenses.

16.Instruct the concerned officials to submit/ furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader with copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete supporting documents, within five (5) days from perfection thereof, for timely review of the legal and technical aspects, reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate the inspection of the projects.

18.The LGU should adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording purchase of supplies and materials. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to prepare and maintain records and reports as required by the NGAS Manual to ensure that the correct amount of inventory and expense accounts are taken up in the books.

19.The LGU should exert its effort to secure the documents necessary to reconstruct the PPE accounts so that depreciation for the current and previous years maybe computed to arrive at the correct valuation of the property, plant and equipment. In case the necessary data could no longer be secured after exhausting all possible means, we recommend that appraisal of the PPEs be considered in order to establish the correct book values.

• Constitute an Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.
• Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to ensure correctness.
Set-up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year as required under Sections 19 (b) and 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Submit to the NDRRMC through the OCD the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF on or before the 5th day following the end of each month and furnish copy to the audit team for audit/evaluation.
We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

The LGU should adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording purchase of supplies and materials. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to prepare and maintain records and reports as required by the NGAS Manual to ensure that the correct amount of inventory and expense accounts are taken up in the books.

The LGU should exert effort to secure the documents necessary to reconstruct the PPE accounts so that depreciation for the current and previous years maybe computed to arrive at the correct valuation of the property, plant and equipment. In case the necessary data could no longer be secured after exhausting all possible means, we recommend that appraisal of the PPEs be considered in order to establish the correct book values.

We advise the Municipal Mayor to strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the designation and bonding of revenue collectors, as prescribed under Section 305 (f) of RA 7160 and Section 65, Volume I of the GAAM. In the meantime, those permanent and properly bonded employees should be directed to take over the issuance of CTCs and Official Receipts and collection of fees and charges.

We recommended that the required monthly fund utilization reports and status of
implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format be immediately posted in the
LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality.

We recommended that Management submit the distribution lists for verification
and validation purposes.

We reiterate our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to:

a. Constitute an Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all its
property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be immediately
prepared and submitted to the Auditor. Items that are beyond economic repair
shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE
Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and conduct regularly
reconciliation of their records to ensure correctness.

We recommended that:

a. the Municipal Accountant obtain the necessary data from the Municipal
Engineer, then prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards for Local Road
Network, provide depreciation separately to each component of the road
network, submit a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation, and
disclose the road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements in
accordance with the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2015-008;

b. the Municipal Mayor constitute a committee to conduct inventory of all road
networks of the Municipality;

c. the Municipal Mayor fill-up the position of Supply Officer or create the
position of GSO; and

d. the Municipal Engineer provide the local accountant and the Supply Officer
with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for
road projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up the RPT and SET
Receivables at the beginning of the year as required under Sections 19 (b) and 20,
NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that:

a. The Municipal Engineer and the MPDC install signboards at the project sites
containing complete information;

b. The Municipal Engineer inform the Office of the Auditor when project
signboard was already installed; and

c. The Municipal Accountant will require as supporting documents the pictures of
signboards being placed in the construction site for claims of mobilization
expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly adhere to the rules and
regulations on the designation and bonding of revenue collectors, as prescribed
under Section 305 (f) of RA 7160 and Section 65, Volume I of the GAAM. In the
meantime, those permanent and properly bonded employees should be directed to
take over the issuance of CTCs and Official Receipts and collection of fees and
charges.

We recommended that the LDRRMO submit to the NDRRMC through the OCD the
monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF on or before the 5th
day following the end of each month and furnish copy to the audit team for
audit/evaluation.

• Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledger cards.
• Require the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare the SSMIs.
• Constitute an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all inventories and to submit a report thereon.
Open a separate depository account and maintain a separate cash book for Special Education Fund.

Conduct regular monitoring of all on-going projects implemented by the LGU and prepare monthly accomplishment reports as to the status of implementation and submit on or before deadline to the Audit Team the Quarterly Accomplishment Report.

The Budget Officer should maintain the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation for each class of expenditure as prescribed to ensure the preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and establish a strong internal control on obligation of expenses.

Instruct the concerned officials to submit/ furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader with copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete supporting documents, within five (5) days from perfection thereof, for timely review of the legal and technical aspects, reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate the inspection of the projects.

We recommend that management submit its GAD Plan and accomplishment reports to facilitate timely evaluation on the implementation of GAD related programs/ projects and substantive audit of GAD related transactions.
The Municipal Treasurer should ensure that all supporting documents of disbursement vouchers are stamped “PAID”.

We reiterate our recommen- dation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be imme- diately prepared and submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year. Items that are beyond eco-nomic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.
We also recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Trea- surer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and to conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to ensure correctness.

The Municipal Assessor should update the Master List of Real Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year.
The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

The LGU should open a separate depository account and maintain a separate cash book for Special Education Fund in compliance with the provisions of Section 310 of RA 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991, and Section 87 of Volume II of the NGAS Manual.

Prepare and submit to the NDRRMC through the OCD the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF on or before the 5th day following the end of each month and furnish copy to the audit team for audit/evaluation.

Conduct regular monitoring of all on-going projects implemented by the municipality and prepare monthly accomplishment reports as to the status of implementation and submit regularly to the Audit Team the Quarterly Accomplishment Report following the format in Annex B of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

The LGU should adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording purchase of supplies and materials. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to prepare and maintain records and reports as required by the NGAS Manual to ensure that the correct amount of inventory and expense accounts are taken up in the books.

The LGU should exert effort to secure the documents necessary to reconstruct the PPE accounts so that depreciation for the current and previous years maybe computed to arrive at the correct valuation of the property, plant and equipment. In case the necessary data could no longer be secured after exhausting all possible means, we recommend that appraisal of the PPEs be considered in order to establish the correct book values.

We advise the Municipal Mayor to strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the designation and bonding of revenue collectors, as prescribed under Section 305 (f) of RA 7160 and Section 65, Volume I of the GAAM. In the meantime, those permanent and properly bonded employees should be directed to take over the issuance of CTCs and Official Receipts and collection of fees and charges.

• Constitute an Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all its
property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be immediately
prepared and submitted to the Auditor. Items that are beyond economic repair
shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.
• Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE
Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and conduct regularly
reconciliation of their records to ensure correctness.

Set-up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year as required
under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

• Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledger cards.
• Require the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare the SSMIs.
• Constitute an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all inventories
and to submit a report thereon.

Open a separate depository account and maintain a separate cash book for Special
Education Fund.

Submit to the NDRRMC through the OCD the monthly Report on the Receipt and
Utilization of the DRRMF on or before the 5th day following the end of each
month and furnish copy to the audit team for audit/evaluation.

Conduct regular monitoring of all on-going projects implemented by the LGU
prepare monthly accomplishment reports as to the status of implementation and
submit on or before deadline to the Audit Team the Quarterly Accomplishment
Report.

The Budget Officer should maintain the Registries of Appro- priation, Allotment and Obligation for each class of expenditure as prescribed to ensure the preparation of the Statement of Compa- rison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and establish a strong internal control on obligation of expenses.

Instruct the concerned officials to submit/ furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader with copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete supporting documents, within five (5) days from perfection thereof, for timely review of the legal and technical aspects, reasona- bleness of the contract costs and to facilitate the inspection of the projects.

We recommend that management submit its GAD Plan and accomplishment reports to facilitate timely evaluation on the implementation of GAD related programs/projects, thereby enabling timely evaluation and audit of GAD related transactions.
The Municipal Treasurer should ensure that all supporting documents of disbursement vouchers are stamped “PAID”.

We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.

We also recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and to conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to ensure correctness.

The Municipal Assessor should update the Master List of Real Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

The LGU should open a separate depository account and maintain a separate cash book for Special Education Fund in compliance with the provisions of Section 310 of RA 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991, and Section 87 of Volume II of the NGAS Manual.
We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

The LGU should adopt the perpetual inventory m e t h o d in recording purchase of supplies and materials. Direct issue the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to prepare and maintain records and reports as required by the NGAS Manual to ensure that the correct amount of inventory and expense accounts are taken up in the books.

We recommend that management submit its GAD Plan and accomplishment reports to facilitate timely eva luation on the implementation of GAD related programs/projects and substantive audit of GAD related transactions.
The Municipal Treasurer should ensure that all supporting documents of disbursement vouchers are stamped 'PAID'.

The LGU should exert effort to secure the documents necessary to reconstruct the PPE accounts so that depreciation for the current and previous years maybe computed to arrive at the correct valuation of the property, plant and equipment. In case the necessary data could no longer be secured after exhausting all possible means, we recommend that appraisal of the PPEs be considered in order to establish the correct book values.

We advise the Municipal Mayor to strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the designation and bonding of revenue collectors, as prescribed under Section 305 (f) of RA 7160 and Section 65, Volume I of the GAAM. In the meantime, those permanent and properly bonded employees should be directed to take over the issuance of CTCs and Official Receipts and collection of fees and charges.

Recommendation:
We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical
inventory of its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be
immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of
each year. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and
dropped from the books.
We also recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards,
respectively, and to conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to ensure
correctness.

Recommendation:
The Municipal Assessor should update the Master List of Real Property
Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation
of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible for the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal
Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section
20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Recommendation:
The LGU should open a separate depository account and maintain a
separate cash book for Special Education Fund in compliance with the provisions
of Section 310 of RA 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of
1991, and Section 87 of Volume II of the NGAS Manual.

Recommendation:
Prepare and submit to the NDRRMC through the OCD the monthly Report on the
Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF on or before the 5th day following the end
of each month and furnish copy to the audit team for audit/evaluation.

Recommendation:
Conduct regular monitoring of all on-going projects implemented by the
municipality and prepare monthly accomplishment reports as to the status of
implementation and submit regularly to the Audit Team the Quarterly
Accomplishment Report following the format in Annex B of the COA Circular No.
2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
R e q uire the Municipal Accountant to revert Other Payables with no valid claim to Accumulated Surplus.

The Budget Officer should maintain the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation for each class of expenditure as prescribed to ensure the preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and establish a strong internal control on obligation of expenses.

Instruct the concerned officials to submit/ furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader with copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete supporting documents, within five days from perfection thereof, for timely review of the legal and technical aspects, reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate the inspection of the projects.
LGU should procure commonly used goods, supplies and materials from the Procurement Service in compliance to the requirements under Section 53(e) of the IRR-A of Republic Act No. 9184.

        We also recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant  and
   Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE  Ledger  Cards  and  Property  Cards,
   respectively, and to conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to

        Require the Municipal Accountant to revert Other Payables  with  no

   The Budget Officer  should  maintain  the  Registries  of  Appropriation,
   Allotment and Obligation for each class of expenditure as  prescribed  to
   ensure the preparation of the  Statement  of  Comparison  of  Budget  and
   Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and establish a strong internal
   control on obligation of expenses.

Recommendation:

        The Municipal Assessor  should  update  the  Master  List  of  Real
   Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an  accurate  basis
   in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and
   the amount due and collectible for the year.  The certified list shall be
   the basis of the Municipal Accountant in  setting  up  the  RPT  and  SET
   Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Recommendation:

        The LGU should open a separate depository account  and  maintain  a

   provisions of Section 310 of  RA  7160,  otherwise  known  as  the  Local
   Government Code of 1991, and Section 87 of Volume II of the NGAS Manual.

   reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate the inspection  of
   the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment (PPE).  Items that are beyond eco-nomic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books. Implement the provi-sions of Section 114 of the NGAS Manual to facilitate accurate recor-ding of the PPE. Recon-ciliation with the Accounting and Trea-surer’s record should be regularly conducted to ensure correctness.

The Municipal     Govern-ment should open a separate depository account and maintain a separate cash book for Special Education Fund in compliance with the provisions of Section 310 of RA 7160, other-wise known as the Local Government Code of 1991, and Section 87 of Volume II of the NGAS Manual.

The Municipal Assessor should update the Mas-ter List of Real Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year.  The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.
Require the Municipal Accountant to revert Other Payables with no valid claim to Retained Operating Surplus.

The LGU should adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording purchase of supplies and materials.  Direct the Municipal Accoun-tant and Treasurer to prepare and maintain records and reports as required by the NGAS Manual to ensure that the correct amount of inventory and expense accounts are taken up in the books.
The Municipal Trea-surer should ensure that all supporting docu-ments of disbursement vouchers are stamped “PAID”.

Transfer Street Sweeper personnel from the Office of the Mayor to the Office of the Muni-cipal Engineer where they rightfully belong to, as maintenance works need technical super-vision that only the Municipal Engineer has competence.
LGU should procure commonly used goods, supplies and materials from the Procurement Service in compliance to the requirements under Section 53(e) of the IRR-A of Republic Act No. 9184.

Recommendation:

        We reiterate our recommendation for the  LGU  to  conduct  periodic
   physical inventory of its property,  plant  and  equipment  (PPE)  and  a
   report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the  Auditor  not
   later than January 31 of each year .   Items  that  are  beyond  economic
   repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.


        We also recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant  and
   Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE  Ledger  Cards  and  Property  Cards,
   respectively, and to conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to
   ensure correctness.

Recommendation:

   We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-
   390 dated November 17, 1992.

Recommendation:

        The Municipal Assessor  should  update  the  Master  List  of  Real
   Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an  accurate  basis
   in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and
   the amount due and collectible for the year.  The certified list shall be
   the basis of the Municipal Accountant in  setting  up  the  RPT  and  SET
   Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Recommendation:

        The LGU should open a separate depository account  and  maintain  a
   separate cash book for Special Education  Fund  in  compliance  with  the
   provisions of Section 310 of  RA  7160,  otherwise  known  as  the  Local
   Government Code of 1991, and Section 87 of Volume II of the NGAS Manual.

Recommendation:

        Require the Municipal Accountant to revert Other Payables  with  no
   valid claim to Accumulated Surplus.

Recommendation:

        The LGU should adopt the perpetual inventory  method  in  recording
   purchase of supplies and materials.  Direct the Municipal Accountant  and
   Treasurer to prepare and maintain records and reports as required by  the
   NGAS Manual to ensure that the correct amount of  inventory  and  expense
   accounts are taken up in the books.

Recommendation:

        We recommend that management submit its GAD Plan and accomplishment
   reports to facilitate timely evaluation  on  the  implementation  of  GAD
   related  programs/projects  and  substantive   audit   of   GAD   related
   transactions.

The LGU should exert effort to secure the documents necessary to reconstruct the PPE accounts so that depre-ciation for the current and previous years maybe computed to arrive at the correct valuation of the property, plant and equipment. In case the necessary data could no longer be secured after exhausting all possible means, we recommend that appraisal of the PPEs be considered in order to establish the correct book values.

We advise the Muni-cipal Mayor to strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the designation and bonding of revenue collectors, as prescribed under Sec-tion 305 (f) of RA 7160 and Section 65, Volume I of the GAAM. In the mean-time, those permanent and properly bonded employees should be directed to take over the issuance of CTCs and Official Receipts and collection of fees and charges.

Require the LGU to established Local Disas-ter Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) in the municipality and a Barangay Disaster Risk Reduction and Manage-ment Office (BDRRMO) in every barangay which shall be responsible for setting the direction, development, implemen-tation and coordination of disaster risk management programs within their territorial jurisdiction.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO   has been established  in CY 2016.
